mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS reflects an active Fed easing cycle that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs for corporates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of concurrent macro weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS fell from 5.25% in Sept 2007 to 2.61% by March 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window during 2008-2024.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below a critical low relative to its trailing 24-month range, it signals an accelerating housing-led demand contraction that historically precedes broader recessions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,100 thousand units and is down more than 30% year-over-year
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1065.0 thousand units in Feb 2008, down sharply from 2006 peaks above 2000
failure_condition: In periods where the HOUST threshold trigger fires, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month decline in PAYEMS and month-over-month rise in UNRATE indicates a labor market inflection point beyond either signal alone, confirming a demand-side recession is underway and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consensus earnings forecasts are cut.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises by at least 0.1pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS declined in Jan-Mar 2008 while UNRATE rose to 5.1% by March 2008 from 4.7% in Nov 2007
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS decline + UNRATE rise events are followed by positive forward 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction signal's Rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from the sum of individual single-series IC contributions.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle where CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated or rising, the Fed's ability to cut further is constrained, limiting the equity-supportive effect of rate cuts and producing negative or muted S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as stagflation fears dominate risk pricing.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 212.687 in Feb 2008 continuing upward trend while FEDFUNDS was cut to 2.61%
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns during periods of simultaneous CPIAUCSL year-over-year increase above 3% and FEDFUNDS decline are positive in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the constrained-easing thesis.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO signals expanding industrial output and supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with near-term corporate revenue strength and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 111.9137 reported Feb 2008 prior to later large downward revisions
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 1-month change and forward 5-day SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS retail sales below a defined threshold indicates weakening consumer demand, a leading indicator of GDP contraction, and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumption-driven earnings outlooks deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 365164 million versus vintage 381416 million reported March 2008, reflecting downward revision to consumer spending
failure_condition: Following two consecutive RSAFS monthly declines, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a rolling 12-month low by more than 0.5 percentage points, it signals the onset of a labor-market-driven recession consistent with Sahm-rule-type dynamics, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as recession probability repricing accelerates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.5pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose to 5.1% in March 2008 from a 2007 low of approximately 4.4%
regime_hint: recession onset via labor market deterioration
failure_condition: After the UNRATE threshold trigger fires, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Aggressive FEDFUNDS rate cuts fail to offset a concurrent sharp decline in HOUST, because monetary easing transmission to housing and credit markets is impaired during balance-sheet stress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days despite the easing signal that would otherwise be bullish.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls more than 1pp over 6 months AND HOUST falls more than 20% year-over-year in the same window
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut from 5.25% to 2.61% between Sept 2007 and March 2008 while HOUST fell to 1065 thousand units, well below prior year levels
regime_hint: impaired monetary transmission during credit crisis
failure_condition: During periods when both trigger conditions are simultaneously met, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, indicating easing dominates over housing weakness contrary to the impaired-transmission hypothesis.
